title: Large-core scheduler evidence corpus
purpose: Define the reproducible large-core scheduler evidence lane before changing scheduler code or claiming swarm-scale speedups.

maintained_benchmark_surface.reason: This is the maintained scheduler decision surface that exercises the Phase 3 ready-work path, local queue fast path, global ready bursts, and scheduler evidence capture overhead.

scenario.description: Collect a comparable baseline for three-lane scheduler decision overhead on a large-core host class without changing scheduler code.
workload_classes: fast-ready dispatch with local priority lock contention

proof_commands: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_xeh8m0_scheduler_evidence CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 RUSTFLAGS='-C debuginfo=1 -C force-frame-pointers=yes' cargo bench -p asupersync --bench scheduler_benchmark -- scheduler/three_lane_decision
closeout_requirements: Run the rch-routed cargo bench command on a host with at least 64 logical CPUs and 256 GiB RAM, or record a no_win verdict that the available worker class cannot prove the claim.
Write receipt_json with the required fingerprint, quantiles, throughput fields, command, git SHA, worker identity, and verdict.
If no improvement is attempted or proven, record verdict=no_win rather than claiming a speedup.
Do not edit scheduler code or README support claims until a measured receipt exists.
not_covered: This artifact does not claim any scheduler speedup.
This artifact does not change scheduler behavior.
This artifact does not prove NUMA placement or worker-affinity changes.
This artifact does not replace later flamegraph, allocation, or off-CPU profiles.
